content.content:
# MariaDB Linux Installation Instructions

Based on the official MariaDB download page, here are the recommended installation methods:

## Package Manager (Preferred Method)

The primary recommendation is to use your Linux distribution's native package manager:

> "MariaDB Server is one of the world's most popular open source relational databases and is available in the standard repositories of all major Linux distributions. Look for the package mariadb-server using the package manager of your operating system."

This means you should search for and install the `mariadb-server` package through your distribution's standard package management tool (apt, yum, dnf, pacman, etc.).

## Alternative Resources

The page acknowledges that while package manager installation is preferred, users have other options available:

- MariaDB Foundation provides official repositories for distributions that don't include the latest versions
- Direct download options are available from their download portal
- A REST API is exposed for automating downloads

## Getting Help

The site encourages users to report issues and seek assistance through their community channels:
- Bug reports on JIRA
- Community discussions on Zulip or mailing lists

The emphasis is clearly on using your OS's native package manager as the simplest and recommended approach.

input.name: linux-f33c81de
input.content:
#!/bin/bash
set -e

echo "Installing MariaDB Server..."

# Update package lists
apt-get update

# Install MariaDB Server (non-interactive)
export DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive
apt-get install -y mariadb-server

# Verify installation
which mysqld
mysqld --version

echo "MariaDB installation completed successfully"

#!/bin/bash
set -e

echo "Starting MariaDB Server..."

# Start MariaDB daemon directly
sudo /usr/sbin/mysqld --user=mysql --datadir=/var/lib/mysql --socket=/var/run/mysqld/mysqld.sock --pid-file=/var/run/mysqld/mysqld.pid --skip-external-locking &

# Wait for service to be ready
sleep 5

# Check if the process is running
if pgrep -x mysqld > /dev/null; then
    echo "✓ MariaDB Server is running!"
else
    echo "✗ Failed to start MariaDB"
    exit 1
fi

echo ""
echo "Connect with: mysql -u root"
echo ""

# Try to connect and show version
echo "Testing connection..."
mysql -u root -e "SELECT VERSION();" 2>/dev/null || echo "Note: MariaDB is running"

echo ""
echo "MariaDB is ready to use"

#!/bin/bash

echo "=== MariaDB Server Launcher ==="
echo ""

# Ensure socket directory exists
sudo mkdir -p /var/run/mysqld
sudo chown mysql:mysql /var/run/mysqld 2>/dev/null || true

# Check if MariaDB is already running
if pgrep -x mysqld > /dev/null 2>&1; then
    echo "✓ MariaDB is already running"
    pgrep -a mysqld | grep mysqld
else
    echo "Starting MariaDB Server..."
    echo ""
    
    # Start MariaDB daemon in background
    sudo /usr/sbin/mysqld --user=mysql --datadir=/var/lib/mysql --socket=/var/run/mysqld/mysqld.sock --pid-file=/var/run/mysqld/mysqld.pid --skip-external-locking > /tmp/mariadb.log 2>&1 &
    
    # Wait for startup
    sleep 3
    
    # Verify it started
    if pgrep -x mysqld > /dev/null 2>&1; then
        echo "✓ MariaDB Server started successfully"
    else
        echo "✗ Failed to start MariaDB"
        cat /tmp/mariadb.log
        exit 1
    fi
fi

echo ""
echo "=== MariaDB Status ==="
pgrep -a mysqld
echo ""
echo "=== Connection Info ==="
echo "Socket: /var/run/mysqld/mysqld.sock"
echo "Port:   3306"
echo "User:   root (needs authorization setup)"
echo ""
mysqld --version
echo ""
echo "=== MariaDB Ready ==="

#!/bin/bash

# Find the MariaDB binary
BINARY_PATH=$(which mysqld)
BINARY_NAME=$(basename "$BINARY_PATH")

# Get version
VERSION=$(mysqld --version | awk '{for(i=1;i<=NF;i++) if($i ~ /[0-9]+\.[0-9]+/) {print $i; exit}}')

# Find desktop entry
DESKTOP_ENTRY=""
if [ -f "/usr/share/applications/mariadb.desktop" ]; then
    DESKTOP_ENTRY="/usr/share/applications/mariadb.desktop"
elif [ -f "/usr/share/applications/mysql.desktop" ]; then
    DESKTOP_ENTRY="/usr/share/applications/mysql.desktop"
fi

# Find icon paths
ICON_PATHS=()

# Check standard icon directories
for icon_dir in /usr/share/icons /usr/share/pixmaps; do
    if [ -d "$icon_dir" ]; then
        # Look for mariadb or mysql icons
        for icon in $(find "$icon_dir" -type f \( -name "*mariadb*" -o -name "*mysql*" \) 2>/dev/null | head -5); do
            ICON_PATHS+=("$icon")
        done
    fi
done

# If no icons found, try to get from desktop file
if [ -z "${ICON_PATHS[@]}" ] && [ -n "$DESKTOP_ENTRY" ] && [ -f "$DESKTOP_ENTRY" ]; then
    ICON_NAME=$(grep "^Icon=" "$DESKTOP_ENTRY" | cut -d= -f2)
    if [ -n "$ICON_NAME" ]; then
        # Try to locate the icon
        ICON_PATH=$(find /usr/share/icons /usr/share/pixmaps -name "$ICON_NAME*" 2>/dev/null | head -1)
        if [ -n "$ICON_PATH" ]; then
            ICON_PATHS+=("$ICON_PATH")
        fi
    fi
fi

# Convert icon array to JSON
ICONS_JSON="["
for ((i=0; i<${#ICON_PATHS[@]}; i++)); do
    ICONS_JSON+="\"${ICON_PATHS[$i]}\""
    if [ $i -lt $((${#ICON_PATHS[@]} - 1)) ]; then
        ICONS_JSON+=","
    fi
done
ICONS_JSON+="]"

# Output JSON metadata
cat <<EOF
{
  "binary_path": "$BINARY_PATH",
  "binary_name": "$BINARY_NAME",
  "display_name": "MariaDB",
  "desktop_entry": ${DESKTOP_ENTRY:+"\"$DESKTOP_ENTRY\"" || "null"},
  "icon_paths": $ICONS_JSON,
  "version": "$VERSION"
}
EOF

#!/bin/bash

echo "=== MariaDB Server Launcher ==="
echo ""

# Ensure socket directory exists
sudo mkdir -p /var/run/mysqld
sudo chown mysql:mysql /var/run/mysqld 2>/dev/null || true

# Check if MariaDB is already running
if pgrep -x mysqld > /dev/null 2>&1; then
    echo "✓ MariaDB is already running"
    pgrep -a mysqld | grep mysqld
else
    echo "Starting MariaDB Server..."
    echo ""
    
    # Start MariaDB daemon in background
    sudo /usr/sbin/mysqld --user=mysql --datadir=/var/lib/mysql --socket=/var/run/mysqld/mysqld.sock --pid-file=/var/run/mysqld/mysqld.pid --skip-external-locking > /tmp/mariadb.log 2>&1 &
    
    # Wait for startup
    sleep 3
    
    # Verify it started
    if pgrep -x mysqld > /dev/null 2>&1; then
        echo "✓ MariaDB Server started successfully"
    else
        echo "✗ Failed to start MariaDB"
        cat /tmp/mariadb.log
        exit 1
    fi
fi

echo ""
echo "=== MariaDB Status ==="
pgrep -a mysqld
echo ""
echo "=== Connection Info ==="
echo "Socket: /var/run/mysqld/mysqld.sock"
echo "Port:   3306"
echo "User:   root (needs authorization setup)"
echo ""

echo ""
echo "=== MariaDB Ready ==="

#!/bin/bash

# Find the MariaDB binary
BINARY_PATH=$(which mysqld)
BINARY_NAME=$(basename "$BINARY_PATH")

# Get version
VERSION=$(mysqld --version | awk '{for(i=1;i<=NF;i++) if($i ~ /[0-9]+\.[0-9]+/) {print $i; exit}}')

# Find display name from package metadata
DISPLAY_NAME=""
if command -v dpkg &>/dev/null; then
    # Use dpkg to get package description
    DISPLAY_NAME=$(dpkg -s mariadb-server 2>/dev/null | grep "^Description:" | cut -d: -f2- | xargs | head -c 100)
fi

# Fallback to default if not found
if [ -z "$DISPLAY_NAME" ]; then
    DISPLAY_NAME="MariaDB"
fi

# Find desktop entry - use package manager to discover it
DESKTOP_ENTRY=""
if command -v dpkg &>/dev/null; then
    # Query package files to find desktop entries
    DESKTOP_ENTRY=$(dpkg -L mariadb-server 2>/dev/null | grep -E "\.desktop$" | head -1)
fi

# If still not found, try common locations
if [ -z "$DESKTOP_ENTRY" ]; then
    for path in /usr/share/applications/mariadb*.desktop /usr/share/applications/mysql*.desktop; do
        if [ -f "$path" ]; then
            DESKTOP_ENTRY="$path"
            break
        fi
    done
fi

# Extract display name from desktop file if available
if [ -n "$DESKTOP_ENTRY" ] && [ -f "$DESKTOP_ENTRY" ]; then
    DESKTOP_NAME=$(grep "^Name=" "$DESKTOP_ENTRY" 2>/dev/null | head -1 | cut -d= -f2)
    if [ -n "$DESKTOP_NAME" ]; then
        DISPLAY_NAME="$DESKTOP_NAME"
    fi
fi

# Find icon paths using package manager
ICON_PATHS=()
if command -v dpkg &>/dev/null; then
    # Find all icon files provided by the package
    while IFS= read -r icon_file; do
        if [ -f "$icon_file" ]; then
            ICON_PATHS+=("$icon_file")
        fi
    done < <(dpkg -L mariadb-server 2>/dev/null | grep -E "\.(png|svg|ico|pixmap)$" | head -10)
fi

# If no icons from package, search standard directories
if [ ${#ICON_PATHS[@]} -eq 0 ]; then
    for icon_dir in /usr/share/icons /usr/share/pixmaps; do
        if [ -d "$icon_dir" ]; then
            # Look for mariadb or mysql icons
            while IFS= read -r icon_file; do
                ICON_PATHS+=("$icon_file")
            done < <(find "$icon_dir" -type f \( -name "*mariadb*" -o -name "*mysql*" \) 2>/dev/null | head -5)
        fi
    done
fi

# Try to extract icon from desktop file
if [ ${#ICON_PATHS[@]} -eq 0 ] && [ -n "$DESKTOP_ENTRY" ] && [ -f "$DESKTOP_ENTRY" ]; then
    ICON_NAME=$(grep "^Icon=" "$DESKTOP_ENTRY" | cut -d= -f2)
    if [ -n "$ICON_NAME" ]; then
        # Try to locate the icon
        ICON_FILE=$(find /usr/share/icons /usr/share/pixmaps -name "$ICON_NAME*" 2>/dev/null | head -1)
        if [ -n "$ICON_FILE" ]; then
            ICON_PATHS+=("$ICON_FILE")
        fi
    fi
fi

# Convert icon array to JSON
ICONS_JSON="["
for ((i=0; i<${#ICON_PATHS[@]}; i++)); do
    ICONS_JSON+="\"${ICON_PATHS[$i]}\""
    if [ $i -lt $((${#ICON_PATHS[@]} - 1)) ]; then
        ICONS_JSON+=","
    fi
done
ICONS_JSON+="]"

# Output JSON metadata
cat <<EOF
{
  "binary_path": "$BINARY_PATH",
  "binary_name": "$BINARY_NAME",
  "display_name": "$DISPLAY_NAME",
  "desktop_entry": $([ -n "$DESKTOP_ENTRY" ] && echo "\"$DESKTOP_ENTRY\"" || echo "null"),
  "icon_paths": $ICONS_JSON,
  "version": "$VERSION"
}
EOF

set -e

echo "Installing MariaDB Server..."

# Update package lists

# Install MariaDB Server (non-interactive)

result.launch_script:
#!/bin/bash

echo "=== MariaDB Server Launcher ==="
echo ""

# Ensure socket directory exists
sudo mkdir -p /var/run/mysqld
sudo chown mysql:mysql /var/run/mysqld 2>/dev/null || true

# Check if MariaDB is already running
if pgrep -x mysqld > /dev/null 2>&1; then
    echo "✓ MariaDB is already running"
    pgrep -a mysqld | grep mysqld
else
    echo "Starting MariaDB Server..."
    echo ""
    
    # Start MariaDB daemon in background
    sudo /usr/sbin/mysqld --user=mysql --datadir=/var/lib/mysql --socket=/var/run/mysqld/mysqld.sock --pid-file=/var/run/mysqld/mysqld.pid --skip-external-locking > /tmp/mariadb.log 2>&1 &
    
    # Wait for startup
    sleep 3
    
    # Verify it started
    if pgrep -x mysqld > /dev/null 2>&1; then
        echo "✓ MariaDB Server started successfully"
    else
        echo "✗ Failed to start MariaDB"
        cat /tmp/mariadb.log
        exit 1
    fi
fi

echo ""
echo "=== MariaDB Status ==="
pgrep -a mysqld
echo ""
echo "=== Connection Info ==="
echo "Socket: /var/run/mysqld/mysqld.sock"
echo "Port:   3306"
echo "User:   root (needs authorization setup)"
echo ""
mysqld --version
echo ""
echo "=== MariaDB Ready ==="
